opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: The pain between my legs is agonizing, it feels like I'm sitting on a golf ball of crushed glass, and it's driving me crazy.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It flared up badly tonight after a really stressful day at work and got much worse after I ejaculated, and I'm terrified it's some kind of severe infection.

symptom_narrative.onset: the pain has come and gone for over 8 months, but it flared up severely tonight
symptom_narrative.duration: a chronic on-and-off problem for 8+ months; this acute flare started earlier this evening
symptom_narrative.character: a deep, crushing, exquisite ache in the area between my legs, like sitting on a golf ball made of crushed glass
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: agonising and driving me crazy — I can't sit down and I've been pacing the room
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: between my legs in the perineum, low down above the pubic bone, and into my lower back
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: worse tonight, and it got noticeably worse after I ejaculated earlier this evening
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing much — plain paracetamol has done almost nothing
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: sitting down, and it got worse after ejaculation and after a very stressful day
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: it burns a bit when I pass urine, I'm needing to go more often and more urgently, but I have NO fever or chills, no shaking, no pain in my back or sides like a stone, and no blood that I can see in my urine.
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've had this same pain flare up on and off for months; I've seen doctors before, had urine tests that came back clear, and been given several courses of antibiotics that never made any difference.
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I can't sit still, I'm pacing the room, and I'm really wound up and anxious about it

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: a long-running on-and-off pain in the same pelvic/perineal area for more than 8 months
past_medical_history.how_managed: seen by several doctors, had urine tests that came back clear, tried multiple antibiotic courses with no benefit

current_medications_as_reported.dose_as_patient_reports: takes it now and then when the pain is bad

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: I have a high-stress desk job and I'd had a really stressful day at work today; I sit for long stretches at a desk. This flare came on after that stress.

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: wants strong pain relief and reassurance, and is worried he needs antibiotics for a serious infection

communication_style.typical_message_style: vivid, dramatic descriptions of the pain; frustrated by the relapsing nature and fearful it is a dangerous infection

objective_data_offered.value: I checked at home and it was normal, no fever, I don't feel hot at all
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading on an uncalibrated home thermometer / self-assessment

objective_data_offered.value: my urine looks a normal colour, I haven't seen any blood in it
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient's visual description of urine, not a laboratory measurement

objective_data_offered.value: I can still pass urine — it stings and I'm going more often, but I'm not blocked up
